items.title: Who is Iran's new ayatollah?
items.standfirst: Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed Iran’s new supreme leader, succeeding his father more than a week after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez reports on the new leader as he now takes over a country in turmoil.

items.title: What to know about Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader
items.standfirst: The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.
items.content: The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.(Image credit: Atta Kenare)

items.title: What will Iran's new Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei be in charge of?
items.standfirst: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has become Iran's new supreme leader. The secretive 56-year-old cleric is only the third supreme leader in the history of the Islamic Republic and has close ties to the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard. But what will his role actually entail?
items.content: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has become Iran's new supreme leader. The secretive 56-year-old cleric is only the third supreme leader in the history of the Islamic Republic and has close ties to the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard. But what will his role actually entail?

Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, Guardian Business and others

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump claims Iran war will be over ‘very soon’ but Tehran says it will determine when
items.standfirst: US president says war is ‘very complete’ and threatens worse strikes if passage of oil via strait of Hormuz is blocked; IRGC says it will not let out ‘one litre of oil’Oil prices drop sharply after Trump moves to reassure marketsTrump says Iran war is ‘very complete, pretty much’ as economic toll risesInvestor hopes for a swift resolution to the Middle East conflict propelled Australian shares higher today, with the benchmark S&P/ASX 200 finishing the day up 1.1% and recovering about $35bn in value after yesterday’s $90bn plunge.Oil prices surged to a four-year high early in the week before coming back down below $US90 a barrel after Donald Trump suggested the Iran conflict would end soon, sending global stock markets higher. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Details on targets hit in Iran, across Middle East during first 10 days of war
items.standfirst: On Monday, President Trump told CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang that the war in Iran "is very complete," but he did not provide a timeline for the end of the conflict. Lindsey Reiser takes a look at the war's first 10 days and some of the strikes that have been carried out across the Middle East.

items.title: Trump tells CBS News "the war is very complete"

items.title: Why has the Iran war sparked fears of stagflation for the global economy?
items.standfirst: Conflict in the Middle East risks knocking growth worldwide and boosting prices, economists warn, amid global market turbulenceMiddle East crisis – live updatesOil prices surged on Monday, triggering a stark sell-off across some of the world’s leading stock markets amid growing concern that the US-Israel war on Iran could set the stage for a global economic shock.While they fell back on Tuesday after Donald Trump suggested the Middle East conflict could end “very soon”, oil continues to trade at high levels. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says Iran war could end 'soon' as Middle East conflict rattles markets
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Monday suggested the war with Iran could end soon but gave no clear timeline for halting attacks that have rattled the Middle East and global markets. Stocks rose after he told CBS the US-Israeli assault was “very complete,” though later remarks offered mixed signals.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Monday suggested the war with Iran could end soon but gave no clear timeline for halting attacks that have rattled the Middle East and global markets. Stocks rose after he told CBS the US-Israeli assault was “very complete,” though later remarks offered mixed signals.

items.title: US stock markets close on high after Iran war drove oil prices above $100 a barrel
items.standfirst: Markets settled after Trump claimed US-Israel war with Iran is ‘very complete’, bringing oil prices down to $85 a barrelHow high could oil prices go – and what might the global economic fallout be?US stock markets closed on a high after oil prices swung wildly on Monday, reaching a four-year high in the morning that rattled Asian and European markets before settling down once Donald Trump said the US-Israel war with Iran is “very complete”.After surging past $100 a barrel on Monday morning, oil prices came down to $85 a barrel by the time that US stock markets closed in the afternoon. US stocks leaped at a report from a CBS News reporter that Trump thinks “the war is very complete, pretty much” because “they have no navy, no communications, they’ve got no air force”. Continue reading...
items.content: Markets settled after Trump claimed US-Israel war with Iran is ‘very complete’, bringing oil prices down to $85 a barrelHow high could oil prices go – and what might the global economic fallout be?US stock markets closed on a high after oil prices swung wildly on Monday, reaching a four-year high in the morning that rattled Asian and European markets before settling down once Donald Trump said the US-Israel war with Iran is “very complete”.After surging past $100 a barrel on Monday morning, oil prices came down to $85 a barrel by the time that US stock markets closed in the afternoon. US stocks leaped at a report from a CBS News reporter that Trump thinks “the war is very complete, pretty much” because “they have no navy, no communications, they’ve got no air force”. Continue reading...

items.title: Why an Iran war inflation shock could wreck global economic recovery
items.standfirst: Central bankers and economists warn prolonged conflict could raise retail prices and rip up growth forecastsHeather Stewart: UK must be prepared for a price shock from the Iran warAn inflation shock triggered by the US-Israel attack on Iran could wreck a fragile global economic recovery that had been expected to gain momentum this year.With oil and gas prices spiking, despite a pledge from Donald Trump to protect tankers making their way through the crucial strait of Hormuz shipping chokepoint, central bankers and economists have warned that a prolonged conflict could increase retail prices around the world and force them to rip up growth forecasts for this year. Continue reading...
items.content: Central bankers and economists warn prolonged conflict could raise retail prices and rip up growth forecastsHeather Stewart: UK must be prepared for a price shock from the Iran warAn inflation shock triggered by the US-Israel attack on Iran could wreck a fragile global economic recovery that had been expected to gain momentum this year.With oil and gas prices spiking, despite a pledge from Donald Trump to protect tankers making their way through the crucial strait of Hormuz shipping chokepoint, central bankers and economists have warned that a prolonged conflict could increase retail prices around the world and force them to rip up growth forecasts for this year. Continue reading...

items.title: CBS News’ Scott MacFarlane leaves network: ‘I look forward to some independence’
items.standfirst: Justice correspondent most known for his January 6 Capitol riot coverage, is latest to quit Bari Weiss-led networkSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxScott MacFarlane, the CBS News justice correspondent most known for his extensive coverage of the January 6 Capitol insurrection, surprised colleagues on Monday morning by announcing his departure from the network, which he joined in late 2021.MacFarlane is only the latest departure from the Bari Weiss-led network, though he said in a memo to colleagues – also posted on LinkedIn – that he personally made the decision to leave. Continue reading...

items.title: Live Nation reaches surprise settlement with justice department in antitrust case
items.standfirst: Live Nation will pay $280m to states in lawsuit, and Ticketmaster will open parts of platform to rival companiesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxLive Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, has reached a surprise settlement with the Department of Justice in its antitrust case just one week after the trial began.Under the agreement, Live Nation will create a $280m settlement fund for states that participated in the lawsuit and Ticketmaster will be required to open parts of its platform to rival ticketing companies, Live Nation announced Monday. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Airline passengers in some of the United States' largest airports are seeing long security lines amid the ongoing partial government shutdown impacting the Department of Homeland Security. clusters.title: Trump claims Iran or ‘somebody else’ could have carried out deadly school strike
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump claims Iran or ‘somebody else’ could have carried out deadly school strike
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, NBC World

items.title: Trump claims Iran or ‘somebody else’ could have carried out deadly school strike
items.standfirst: President makes evidence-free claim despite video showing US Tomahawk missile hit naval base next to schoolAs oil prices surged amid the widening war with Iran, Donald Trump suggested, without evidence, on Monday that the strike on an Iranian elementary school could have been carried out by Iran or “somebody else”.During back-to-back appearances in Florida, Trump was asked whether the US would accept responsibility for a strike that hit the school and killed scores of people, many of them children, after video evidence showed a US Tomahawk struck the naval base next to it. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
clusters.neutral_headline: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges

items.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
items.standfirst: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.
items.content: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.

items.title: Jailed Istanbul mayor goes on trial in corruption case slammed as political
items.standfirst: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.
items.content: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.
